metadata_information.title: Human capital and the changing structure of the Indian economy
metadata_information.version: Python script, from D&R API, generated on 2026-03-19

title_statement.title: Human capital and the changing structure of the Indian economy

document_description.abstract: Using panel data for the fourteen major states of India over the 1980-2000 period, the authors estimate the effect of human capital endowment on the performance of the state economies. They find that greater availability of skilled workers had a positive and significant impact on output in the service sectors. They do not find any such effect for the manufacturing sectors. The paper shows that the differential effect on services and manufacturing arises because service sectors are more skill intensive.

An objective of the series is to get the findings out quickly, even if the presentations are less than fully polished. The papers carry the names of the authors and should be cited accordingly.
